what_it_does: Agent-facing CSS governance file for the styles/ directory that adds token conventions and a load-order cascade on top of the root CLAUDE.md.
observations: colors_and_type.css is the only file where raw hex values are permitted
All other CSS and JS must reference var(--token) and mirror tokens in src/tokens.js
Token families are centralized in :root and include accent, ink/paper, surfaces, status, hairline, spacing, radius, shadow, type, tracking, font, and motion tokens
Surfaces are semantic zone washes driven by a single --zone-strength dial and referenced via JS, panel classes, and surfaces.css
The cascade order is explicitly listed: colors_and_type.css -> app.css + surfaces.css + per-surface files -> theme.css -> conformity.css -> responsive.css
Cross-cutting safety and responsive rules are restricted to conformity.css and responsive.css
One-off rgba alphas for effects are allowed as distinct effect values rather than tokenized colors
theme.css recolours the gold accent system live
standouts: Single-source token principle with one file allowed to contain raw hex values
Explicit load-order cascade where the last loaded file wins
Semantic surface system controlled by one --zone-strength dial and wired across CSS and JS
conventions: CSS tokens mirrored manually in src/tokens.js
Layered CSS architecture with precedence resolved by load order, not specificity
No duplication of rules earlier in the cascade to override later layers
Bespoke effect alphas are treated as effect values, not color tokens
concerns: No enforcement mechanism is described for the single raw-hex rule beyond convention
The inline list of per-surface files may be incomplete or drift over time
notes: This file tells agents how the CSS token system, surface semantics, and cascade layers are organized in styles/.
questions: What is the exact token inventory and value set in colors_and_type.css?
How is --zone-strength defined and how are surfaces wired in surfaces.css?
Is src/tokens.js generated from colors_and_type.css or maintained as a manual mirror?
What specific rules does conformity.css contain beyond the listed items?
What is the mechanism and trigger for the live accent recolour in theme.css?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches stated purpose: it documents CSS token and cascade conventions for the styles directory.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for design tokens
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a maintainable, agent-discoverable CSS architecture where tokens, surfaces, and a strict load order prevent color duplication and keep overrides in predictable layers.
novelty: Explicitly forbids raw hex outside one file and resolves cascade precedence by load order rather than specificity or duplicated rules.
contribution: It contributes a documented token-and-cascade contract for the styles layer: a single raw-hex source, a mirrored JS token file, a strict load order, and a semantic surface system that a fused system would keep as its CSS governance layer.
summary_for_embedding: AGENTS.md in styles/ documents CSS token conventions, the single-file raw-hex rule, token families, and the load-order cascade for the counterpart project's stylesheets.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to guide agents in maintaining a consistent, layered CSS token system and to prevent ad-hoc color duplication or cascade-order overrides.
